text: The surgical setup includes port placement for robotic piloplasty: the camera is positioned in the left suprapubic region, slightly left of the midline. Additional ports are located in the right inguinal crease, umbilicus, and epigastrium, with the patient in a semi-lateral position.
Upon entering the abdomen, the camera provides a view of the kidney from below, with the lower pole positioned closer to the viewer.

The surgical field centers on the pelvis, with two robotic arms approaching from either side. The triangulation is positioned slightly inferior to the kidney, with the lower pole of the kidney being the first anatomical structure encountered.
The surgeon observes a slightly posteriorly rotated kidney with a non-dilated pelvis due to obstruction. The hepatic flexure is adhered to the lateral abdominal wall and requires careful removal.
The surgeon hitches up the pelvis, revealing a vein leading to the lower pole that restricts further mobilization to avoid damaging the vessel.
The ureter is clearly visible in the surgical field, easily identifiable among the surrounding anatomical structures.
The surgeon begins the PUJ anastomosis following the dismemberment, with the surgical field showing clear dissection lines and exposed anatomical structures.

The surgeon completes suturing of the posterior wall using robotic instruments, demonstrating a range of movement similar to hand suturing. A stent is placed, and preparation begins for the closure of the anterior wall.
